dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires bar range expansion relative to recent average (volatility ratio). Mapped using 'range' (high-low) compared to atr_14 as proxy for volatility threshold. Used pivot as support/resistance level substitute. Volume confirmation uses hvol_20 (20-bar historical volatility). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both breakouts above (LONG) and below (SHORT) levels. Assumed 2% stop-loss and 20-day max hold as reasonable defaults for breakout trades.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires bar range expansion relative to recent average (volatility ratio). Mapped using 'range' (high-low) compared to atr_14 as proxy for volatility threshold. Used pivot as support/resistance level substitute. Volume confirmation uses hvol_20 (20-bar historical volatility). Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal applies to both breakouts above (LONG) and below (SHORT) levels. Assumed 2% stop-loss and 20-day max hold as reasonable defaults for breakout trades.

source_rule_text: A valid breakout that initiates an extended trend is characterized by increased volatility measured as the expansion of individual bar ranges relative to recent price action history.
